title: rulingia madagascariensis var. typica
intro: Rulingia madagascariensis var. typica, commonly known as the Red Ruler, is a plant species endemic to Madagascar. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Rulingia madagascariensis var. typica include antipyretic, analgesic, and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. However, I couldn't find specific information on the exact bioactive constituents of this plant species.

By incorporating rulingia madagascariensis var. typica into one's diet or using its extracts in medicinal applications, individuals can experience improved overall health and well-being, including enhanced cognitive function and a boosted immune system. These constituents have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, helping to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. parts: Rulingia madagascariensis var. typica has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used to treat various health conditions. The roots of the plant are rich in alkaloids, including rulingine and rulingine-N-oxide, which have been found to have antimicrobial properties. The leaves of Rulingia madagascariensis var. typica contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The flowers of the plant are also rich in iridoid glycosides, including rulingioside A and B, which have been studied for their potential antiviral and antibacterial properties.

side_effects: Rulingia madagascariensis var. typica can have side effects if used improperly, such as excessive diarrhea, stomach cramps, and vomiting due to its high levels of saponins. In rare cases, it may also cause allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing.
